title: Support configurable ginkgo --timeout in `winmake.ps1`
body:
### Feature request description

Now timeout for the localmachine test suite is hard coded https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9f7af81cf8a77f911c9843b1ed88ab8167874ace/winmake.ps1#L89

The value set is 50m, which is not enough for lower spec runners, because of the number of tests in this suite.

### Suggest potential solution

Allow to override it with environment variable as in `Makefile` https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9f7af81cf8a77f911c9843b1ed88ab8167874ace/Makefile#L143C1-L143C30

The complication is that there is discrepancy between timeout values in `Local-Unit` and `Local-Machine` functions.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I'm currently applying patch on top of the checkout to change this, but having an ability to configure it within build job would make this cleaner.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: I don't mind an env var for this. These tests require fast IO as the big machine image copies seems to be bottle necked mainly by IO and not cpu/memory, at least for our CI.

> These tests require fast IO 

This is indeed an issue running machine tests inside VMs with nested virtualization enabled. Even when there is enough compute, IO will be lacking.

I will create PR for this change. Probably after 5.6 is released to not distract team for this minor change.
